We performed a novel case where a tumor in the apex of the right chest was found to be arising from the T1 nerve root.
This was a schwannoma, which is a benign tumor.

Therefore, we elected to perform a novel excision of the tumor by making an incision within the tumor bed on the capsule of the tumor, excising the tumor from the capsule, leaving the capsule in place to spare the T1 nerve root.
We were able to enucleate the tumor from the capsule, sparing
the T1 nerve root and sparing the patient from a Horner's syndrome.
This unique approach was popularized by Dr. Spinner, one of our neurosurgeons on staff at Mayo Clinic.
